reports.executiveSummary: Progress was made in preparing the transition to the first in-country phase of the project. Coordination continued with the Belize Olympic Committee regarding accommodation options, travel planning and operational readiness. Information provided by the Olympic Committee was reviewed to support future engagement with National Federations. Initial review activities also began regarding athlete qualification and competition opportunities related to the Central American and Caribbean Games. The main challenge during the week remained the confirmation of suitable accommodation arrangements to support the start of on-site activities
reports.supportNeeded: Continued coordination and support regarding travel arrangements and the transition to the first in-country phase of the project.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Finalize travel arrangements, begin the first on-site phase of the project, and continue the review of athlete eligibility, qualification status and competition opportunities for the Central American and Caribbean Games.

reports.executiveSummary: The week focused on providing technical support to the Olympic Committee regarding Athletics qualification for the Central American and Caribbean Games. Consultation with the Games Technical Director and the International Technical Delegate for Athletics clarified qualification scenarios and supported the preparation of technical recommendations for the Olympic Committee. In parallel, travel arrangements and the initial implementation framework were completed in preparation for the first in country phase of the project.
reports.supportNeeded: Continue providing operational support during the transition to the first in country phase of the project and maintain coordination as implementation begins.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Begin the first in country phase of the project, establish the initial work agenda, become familiar with the Olympic Committee's operations and gain a deeper understanding of the Belize sport system and its key stakeholders.

reports.executiveSummary: The first in country phase of the project focused on establishing the institutional and operational conditions required to begin implementation. Initial meetings with the leadership of the Belize Olympic and Commonwealth Games Association aligned expectations, clarified the working approach and strengthened institutional coordination. Participation in the National Sports Council meeting provided valuable insight into the country's sport planning process and created the opportunity to contribute technical input on the strategic role of the National Competition System as a key driver of athlete development, federation strengthening and long term sport system sustainability. The week also included the review of institutional programmes and operational information, providing valuable context to support the implementation of future technical activities.
reports.supportNeeded: Continue monitoring the project's initial implementation and provide guidance as needed during my integration into the Belize sport system.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Initiate technical engagement with National Federations, strengthen institutional relationships through field activities and begin implementing the technical work plan in collaboration with the Belize Olympic and Commonwealth Games Association.

reports.executiveSummary:
Progress this week focused on strengthening National Federation planning, institutional coordination, and Olympic education initiatives. A strategic planning session with the DOC Executive reviewed the draft National Sport Strategy, minimum federation compliance standards, and a proposed National Federation Support Office. Executive members supported the concept of a federation support function within the DOC, subject to available funding.

Capacity-building sessions for Federation Presidents, General Secretaries, and Coaches advanced Operations Plans, Shared Calendars, High Performance Plans, Athlete Development Pathways, and Olympic Month preparations. Coaches also received guidance on planning, periodization, and future Panam Sports coach education opportunities.

A strategic meeting with the Minister of State with responsibility for Youth and Sports advanced discussions on collaboration, OVEP implementation, Olympic Month activities, and a proposed MOU between the Ministry and DOC. New logistical challenges concerngin training emerged requiring follow up discussions with federations and DOC.
reports.supportNeeded: -Continued follow-up regarding the proposal and budget for a Local Sport Methodologist and support in approval of relay budget for OVEP once submitted on Relay (currently in final review with DOC President)
reports.nextWeekFocus: The main focus for next week is the successful delivery of the first Olympic Month school visits to inspire youth participation in sport, promote Olympic values, and raise awareness of the Dominica Olympic Committee and open doors to further collaboration.

reports.executiveSummary:
This week, I conducted Olympic Day school outreach visits with Dominica Olympic Committee staff at five primary schools, promoting Olympic values, athlete development pathways, and opportunities available through National Federations. I continued follow-up with National Federations regarding outstanding operational, high-performance, and athlete pathway planning documents, resulting in confirmed or tentative working sessions with Cycling, Aquatics, and Basketball, with additional follow-up underway with Athletics and Volleyball.

Preparations advanced for the OVEP Teacher Training Workshop and National Federation Safeguarding Workshop through coordination with facilitator Silvia Dalotto, Ministry of Education stakeholders, and local safeguarding partners. Follow-up correspondence to government stakeholders and National Federations was reviewed and approved by DOC leadership. I also promoted Panam Sports coach development opportunities through media outreach with DOC and communication with national federations. I also consulted with a prospective sport psychologist regarding a cost-effective pilot project to support Dominican athletes.
reports.supportNeeded: Thank you for approving the budget for the Local Sport Methodologist position within the Dominica Olympic Committee and for the guidance throughout the OVEP Workshop planning process. At this time, no additional support or coordination is required. I will continue advancing the approved activities.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Continue Olympic Day school visits, conduct follow-up meetings with National Federations to advance operational and high-performance planning documents, consult with the DOC Treasurer on federation financial management templates, and continue preparations for OVEP and safeguarding workshops.

reports.executiveSummary: This week, priority work advanced in support of the Dominica Olympic Committee's institutional, technical, and athlete-support objectives. Collaboration was strengthened with the Ministtries of education and sport through school principals and ports Division leadership engagment in preparation for the OVEP Teacher Training Workshop. Follow-up with 58 school principals and the Sports Division lead supported participant confirmation and emphasized the importance of selecting committed representatives. Federation capacity-building progressed through individualized sessions and scheduling of follow up sessions. Safeguarding readiness also advanced through collection of Safeguarding Officer appointment forms, follow-up with federations still submitting forms, and initiation of the July 4 workshop attendance list. High-performance support was explored through receipt and submission of a sport psychology quote for review by the Capacity Officer and DOC President. Key challenges remain around federation availability for administrative work, school follow-up demands, and delayed confirmation of a meeting with the Massacre facility coordinator.
reports.supportNeeded: Support from Panam Sports would be appreciated in facilitating a brief strategic learning meeting with Jimena Saldaña and, if possible, Sara Rosario, either jointly or separately, to help align and adjust Dominica's federation support model with regional best practices.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Confirming OVEP and Safeguarding workshop participant names, coordinating workshop details with facilitators, exploring shared facility use for affiliates, continuing public engagement, supporting National Federations, and training Staci Hill for federation support.

reports.executiveSummary: This week, progress continued across Olympic education, federation capacity-building, stakeholder engagement, and early facility coordination. Olympic Day celebrations brought together National Federations, schools, families and community members, strengthening DOC visibility and promoting the Olympic values of friendship, respect and excellence. OVEP preparation required extensive follow-up with school principals, education stakeholders, the Public Service Union, Panam Sports and Ms. Silvia Dalotto to confirm participants and coordinate logistics. Federation support advanced through a session on transparent selection criteria, individual sessions with Volleyball and Athletics, follow-up communication to all federations, and continued training of Staci Hill as the local federation support person. Facility visits and inquiries helped clarify booking processes, access challenges and possible shared-use options. Key challenges included slower responses from some schools and less active federations, delays in facility coordination with external stakeholders, and postponed discussion on high-performance sport psychology support pending DOC leadership guidance.
reports.supportNeeded: Support from Panam Sports through the planned meeting with Jimena Saldaña would help confirm alignment of the local federation support role and advise on its title/framing. Staci Hill is being trained through templates, meeting exposure and shadowing to gradually support federations independently.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Federation follow-up meetings and local sport methodologist/federation support training through shadowing; final OVEP/Safeguarding confirmations, certificates and logistics; press release distribution; facility-use recommendations and marketing support to promote the Panam coach course.

reports.executiveSummary: This week focused on final preparation for the Safeguarding Workshop and Olympic Values Education Programme Workshop, while continuing institutional capacity-building work with the DOC and National Federations. Time was dedicated to facilitator communication, participant coordination, support documents, and linking both workshops to governance, safeguarding, Olympic values, and youth sport development. A second focus was training the Federation Support Officer and establishing workflows for federation follow-up, document review, shared folders, reporting, and qualification pathway research. This supports federations in developing stronger high-performance and athlete pathway objectives. Capacity-building continued through follow-up with football, swimming, cycling, athletics, and cricket stakeholders, supporting relationship-building and next steps. Communication was sent to the Minister of Sports, DOC President, and Massacre facility manager regarding the urgent facility issue. Support was also provided to the Olympic Academy through agenda development, Barbados Olympic Academy connection, and follow up meetings with Thea Lafond and National Scholar Dr. Honychurch.
reports.supportNeeded: I have developed a rough draft for responsiblities for federation support (and if appropriate, olympic academy lisason). I would really appreciate some feedback on this. I have am also developing a dashboard for progreses reporting by federation support officer and would appreciate feecback there.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Olympic Academy next steps and DOC support; OVEP workshop implementation and measurement; marketing team workflow; Federation Support Officer workflow and progress dashboard; AGM preparation and methodology progress report; marketing strategy and strategic planning recommendations.

reports.executiveSummary: For this week just define on time the schedule for the training with the associations
reports.nextWeekFocus: Complete the program of the training with the association's

reports.executiveSummary:
Challenge don't have the scheduled meetings for the training of the associations

Coordinate efforts with administrative from the NOC, Specifics tasks were determined, defining people with specific tasks.

Achievement Carry out the training adapting to their available times
reports.supportNeeded: Schedule meetings together
reports.nextWeekFocus: Continue with the training, still missing 13 associations

So, we get the goal for this week because we have 3 training sessions, so it's ok. If we have less than 3 it's mean we are not doing the correct work to involve the association in this process.
reports.supportNeeded: Follow up the program that had already sent on February and on May
reports.nextWeekFocus: Im looking for 4 training sessions, we started working on, this currently week

The goal for the week was handle 4 training sessions, but it couldn't be
This week identified some other's weakness points in dailys activities. Communication skills and process management for Shivon
The areas of opportunity is how we can make efficiently in my dailys task

reports.nextWeekFocus: Visit some training sessions, work so closely with Shivon in process management and some tools that help her organize her activities

Analyzing the dynamics of the programming the strategy focused on being more flexible but coordinating pending meetings directly with Veda.
It was successful, we had an inmediate response from the associations.
The next week I am looking to work closely with Shivon i process management

This week focused on active field diagnostics, grassroots monitoring, and administrative alignment. I conducted an on-site audit of the weightlifting program, identifying severe logistical challenges including the lack of a dedicated facility and the heavy administrative burden on the coach, compounded by their current SLOC observer status. Over the weekend, I provided direct operational assistance and monitored youth talent ID metrics during the two-day Athletics National Age Group Championship in Soufrière. Administratively, I finalized this new reporting format with Panam Sports reps and initiated collaborative workflow discussions with the newly appointed NOC methodologist to ensure our roles are complementary. A critical institutional gap remains the pending funding confirmation from SLOC and Panam Sports for the upcoming June Coaches Conference and the Elite Athlete Medical Support project, which urgently requires executive approval to avoid logistical delays.
reports.supportNeeded: Due to delayed SLOC communication and the President's travel, urgent Panam Sports budget approval or input is needed for the June Conference and Medical Project. Delivering these tangible benefits now is critical to cement federation buy-in and trust before transitioning to remote support.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Primary priorities include securing project funding approvals from SLOC/Panam Sports, holding follow-up alignment meetings with the new NOC methodologist, executing a technical field diagnostic audit for Swimming, and assisting national federations with monthly reporting compliance.

reports.executiveSummary: While progress continues on digital integration, a significant administrative gap persists regarding institutional support for the federations. To optimize high-performance outcomes, it is essential to align NOC expectations with enhanced preparatory resources. Addressing this misalignment is critical to securing federation buy-in, as they require tangible support structures to successfully execute the technical requirements of this project.
reports.supportNeeded: I request institutional advocacy to bridge the gap between NOC expectations and federation support. Expedited budget approval for the Coaches Conference and medical project is vital to secure federation trust and project buy-in before transitioning to remote tracking.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Launching the weekly coach visit schedule (June 15th). Finalizing logistics for the June 27th Coaches Conference. Conducting integrated technical diagnostics with the new football methodologist to unify high-performance monitoring across all federations.

reports.executiveSummary: This week prioritized field diagnostics for Boxing, Table Tennis, and Weightlifting, and finalizing preparations for the June 27th Coaches Conference. The SLOC President successfully secured reaffirmed pledges from federation executives. Despite operational success, challenges remain: the project relies heavily on individual initiative rather than functioning as a systemic SLOC mandate, and conference budget cuts misalign with high executive expectations for regional results. Finally, to address critical gaps in athlete competition exposure, we are proposing a regional solution utilizing the international methodologist network to host preparatory training camps.
reports.supportNeeded: We appreciate the presenter and certificates. Moving forward, we request coordination to leverage the Panam Sports methodologist network to organize regional, multi-country training camps. This is critical to provide our athletes with the high-level competition exposure they currently lack.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Execute a successful June 27th Coaches Conference despite material limitations. Foster stronger institutional alignment between SLOC and federations post-event. Continue utilizing the field-visit cadence to onboard coaches and athletes into the central monitoring database framework.

reports.executiveSummary: The primary achievement this week was the highly successful execution of the June 27th Coaches Conference. The event saw excellent turnout, and qualitative feedback indicated strong participant satisfaction with both the technical information and its delivery. This success provides critical leverage to address our ongoing institutional challenges. Moving forward, we are utilizing the trust built at the conference to drive federation compliance for the Elite Athlete and Coaches Database. Additionally, we are pushing forward on our proposed solution to the athlete exposure deficit by seeking to activate the Panam Sports methodologist network for regional training camps.
reports.supportNeeded: We request active coordination support to connect with other NOC methodologists. Facilitating these introductions is essential to begin organizing the regional, multi-country training camps proposed last week to increase our athletes' competition exposure.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Priorities include post-conference follow-ups to drive athlete and coach registrations into the central database, conducting a strategic review with SLOC leadership to institutionalize the program, and initiating planning for the proposed regional training camps.

reports.executiveSummary: We are in the final stages of completing the contract for the implementation of the school physical education program, with a one-month delay, awaiting acceptance of the final version for signature and project commencement.
reports.supportNeeded: If we are unable to make progress in this process by the end of this week, I will need support to access the NOC and government more effectively.
reports.nextWeekFocus: If the goal is achieved, focus on meetings with the Ministry of Sport and Education to resolve outstanding issues, as the planned agenda is already behind schedule.

reports.executiveSummary: Throughout the week, we awaited a response from the SKN Attorney General regarding the latest version of the contract for the school physical education implementation program in SKN and the university-level training of 50 teachers. We followed up with the relevant stakeholders for a response and met with the SKNOC to review timelines and consider next steps or potential modifications.
reports.supportNeeded: At this stage, given the requirements involved, there is no need for Panam's involvement to assist with this process.
reports.nextWeekFocus: As next week is the last of the month, we hope the return date projected by the Ministry of Sport is met so that we can move forward with this outstanding monthly matter.

reports.executiveSummary:
1- We have made progress with the workshop materials, both for federation governance and the Level 0 course for coaches.
2- We have managed to visit at least three different sports and athletes with Olympic scholarships.
3- We have contacted the coaches of athletes with Olympic scholarships and scheduled meetings for next week.
4- We have been very persistent in emailing the federations to request their training schedules.
reports.supportNeeded: At this stage, We will continue to build bridges of communication. We firmly believe in the workshops and that they will have a positive impact. Thank you very much. We continue to move forward for the Glory of Sport in Suriname🇸🇷. Thank you PANAM
reports.nextWeekFocus: We are continuing with the paperwork, meetings with coaches, and coordinating the training follow-up in Paramaribo.

reports.executiveSummary: We were able to obtain draft versions of the governance and strategic planning workshops, as well as a workshop for coaches, tailored specifically for Suriname. Thanks to Tony Mesa's support, we coordinated with a badminton coach from the Dominican Republic; the idea was to organize a training camp in the Dominican Republic to help Rivano Bisphan arrive at CACSO better prepared. We have also been able to speak with the coach of another athlete with an Olympic scholarship, Tachana Dalger—track cycling. The coach is James Hey, a British national with a long career as a coach. Finally, Mike Nahar was able to attend the training session of D'Angelo Huisden, an Olympic-scholarship track and field athlete residing in Suriname who, due to weather conditions, had been unable to train at his usual track. We continue working for the sporting glory of Suriname.
reports.supportNeeded: We are requesting support from Panam Sports to purchase training equipment and tickets for the World Championships in Shanghai in October 2026, as part of the preparation for Olympic scholarship athlete Tachana Dalger. Please provide information on the process for applying for this support.
reports.nextWeekFocus: We continue to work on governance and strategic planning workshops, coaching courses, and the monitoring and evaluation of training for athletes with Olympic scholarships who are preparing for CACSO. Prepare a proposal for the purchase of tickets for the Track Cycling World Championships.

reports.executiveSummary: Having the support of Tachana's coach is a sign that she is well guided by a professional who is committed to her comprehensive training, especially since she is living in Trinidad and Tobago; that is why I consider it an achievement to have drafted a document to secure support from the various organizations within the Olympic structure. We still have a meeting pending with the other athlete in Trinidad and Tobago, Xavi Wadilie, and we will continue to follow up. That said, Tachana's proposal is currently with the SOC Secretary General, so we are awaiting a response before we can send it to Panam Sports and Olympic Solidarity. Prior to this project, there was no Methodology Department at the SOC, so we are establishing this professional unit. Administrative staff and members of the Executive Committee have already taken on roles within the Official Games themselves; however, there was no support for the preparation process, and that is what we are focusing on now.
reports.supportNeeded: Panam Sports, has created this project to support the development of Caribbean countries; we have made progress and have been better heard. We need financial support to prepare athletes for the 2028 Los Angeles Games and, of course, for the next cycle, the 2032 Brisbane Olympic Games.
reports.nextWeekFocus: 1- We continue to monitor the Tachana project. 2- We reviewed administrative aspects of the call for applications for the governance and strategic planning workshops, the coaching course, and the monitoring of athletes' training. 3- Second trip to Suriname from June 26 to August 9.

reports.executiveSummary: I am grateful to Panam Sports for bringing me back to Suriname to continue with Phase 2 of the Development Plan's implementation. Moving forward with proposals based on the athletes' needs has been a slow process, but it is vitally important to understand their realities in order to make the necessary adjustments. In short, establishing communication with athletes and coaches has been a slow process, but we believe we have now succeeded in hearing from the athletes and coaches—with the exception of Jair Tjon En Fa, Xavi Wadilie's coach.
reports.supportNeeded: Support in securing funding so that our athletes can receive the best possible training on their journey to the 2028 Los Angeles Olympic Games. We also hope that, through PANAM, we can connect with Olympic Solidarity or other international organizations that could also help our athletes.
reports.nextWeekFocus:
Secure logistical support from the SOC.
Confirm registrations for the two workshops.
Follow up on in-person and remote training sessions.

reports.executiveSummary:
1- Back in Suriname: new accommodations, car rental, and office operations.
2- We're moving forward with the workshop's implementation and are awaiting the federations' participation.
3- We divided up the training sessions, and I visited the training sessions for the two sports assigned to me.
4- Mike and I reviewed the status of the four project initiatives and their expected outcomes.
5- I participated in the Mental Health and Safe Sports Workshop (Saturday, July 4, 10 a.m.–1 p.m. – SOC).
reports.supportNeeded: I have doubts about my role and impact on the project.
reports.nextWeekFocus:
1- Workshop on governance and strategic planning.
2- Visit to training sessions for the assigned sports.
3- Resume work on the federation rankings.

reports.executiveSummary: Since May 13, 2026, little progress has been made with the LA28 planning initiatives and the 2036 Strategic Planning process. The lack of support or even recognition from the Ministry of Sport and Sport TT has delayed the entire project. While the overall plan for the delivery in the fall, time has been lost over the last month and my contract expired on May 31, 2026.
reports.supportNeeded: A one-on-one meeting with Emilio and Jimena to discuss next steps.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Secure a contract for June-December 2026 and restart Phase 3 of the project.

reports.executiveSummary:
As identified in December 2025 and reenforced in May/June 2026, the TT athlete funding model is TT is broken and needs to be fixed. This solely rests in the hands of the Ministry of Sport and Sport TT.

Despite this challenge, three main projects have been identified to address both a 1. tactical (LA28) and 2. strategic (2036) approach and to take advantage of a once in a lifetime opportunity to capitalize on the Summer Olympic Games in North America in LA28. With the addition of a 3. local methodologist, the entire structure of Olympic sport in TT can be reshaped to unleash to their true potential.
reports.supportNeeded: If the meeting on July 1 with the Ministry of Sport & SportTT is not successful, TTOC is going to need to support from PANAM Sports and the IOC.
reports.nextWeekFocus: Sign the TTOC & PANAM Sports contract and start to address the three main projects.
